description: Split the set-level relational-model pass factory into permissive and strict modes. Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/RelationalModelSetPasses.cs` so `CreateDefault()` excludes `ValidateCollectionSemanticIdentityPass`, and add `CreateStrict()` or an equivalent explicit strict builder mode that appends `ValidateCollectionSemanticIdentityPass` immediately after `SemanticIdentityCompilationPass`. Acceptance: the shared/default pipeline still compiles semantic-identity metadata and downstream stable-identity constraints when metadata exists, but it no longer hard-fails generic persisted multi-item scopes that lack compiled semantic identity; the strict pipeline preserves deterministic fail-fast behavior for supported-model and runtime-boundary callers.
steps-to-verify: Add or update `RelationalModelSetPassesTests` to assert both pass orders and to prove strict mode contains `ValidateCollectionSemanticIdentityPass` immediately after `SemanticIdentityCompilationPass`.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Keep DDL and hand-authored generic fixture compilation on the permissive relational-model pipeline. Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl/DdlPipelineHelpers.cs`, any DDL fixture builders, and related regression coverage so DDL intentionally consumes `CreateDefault()` rather than the strict pass set. Acceptance: DDL compilation succeeds for generic fixtures such as `small/ext` and `small/nested` even when they omit `arrayUniquenessConstraints`; emitted DDL still reflects stable-key metadata and compiled semantic identity when present; and this story does not reintroduce collection semantic-identity validation as a universal DDL gate.
steps-to-verify: Add or update DDL regression coverage for `small/ext` and `small/nested` so fixture setup no longer fails on `$.addresses[*]` merely because semantic identity is absent.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj`

category: Strict Validation Coverage And DMS-1103 Story Update
description: Make the supported-model boundary explicit in both code and story documentation for DMS-1103. Update the validation-focused relational-model tests to opt into `CreateStrict()` explicitly, and revise `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/11-stable-collection-row-identity.md` so the acceptance criteria and tasks state that semantic-identity fail-fast applies at the strict/runtime boundary rather than every shared compile path. Acceptance: negative semantic-identity tests still fail with the existing deterministic diagnostics under strict mode; DMS-1103 explicitly documents that the story introduces the strict validation capability without requiring `CreateDefault()` to reject generic out-of-bound fixtures.
steps-to-verify: Update `CollectionSemanticIdentityValidationPassTests` and any other negative semantic-identity fixtures to construct the strict pass list explicitly and assert the same diagnostics as before.

Inspect `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/01-relational-model/11-stable-collection-row-identity.md` and confirm it distinguishes the permissive shared pipeline from the strict supported-model/runtime boundary.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/02-ddl-emission/07-stable-collection-row-identity-ddl.md` so DMS-1107 explicitly depends on the permissive relational-model build contract from DMS-1103. Acceptance: the DDL story states that it consumes compiled stable-identity metadata when present, but it does not own or reintroduce global semantic-identity validation for hand-authored generic fixtures; any statement about fail-fast behavior is scoped to supported-model/runtime-boundary callers rather than universal DDL compilation.
steps-to-verify: Review `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/02-ddl-emission/07-stable-collection-row-identity-ddl.md` and confirm the story no longer implies that DDL should fail universally on missing semantic identity.
Run: `rg -n "strict|default|semantic identity|runtime" reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/02-ddl-emission/07-stable-collection-row-identity-ddl.md`

description: Carry the strict semantic-identity contract into the first executable consumer story. Update `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/04b-stable-collection-merge-plans.md` and the eventual implementation in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RuntimeMappingSetCompiler.cs` so runtime/write-plan compilation opts into `CreateStrict()` instead of depending on `CreateDefault()` being globally strict. Acceptance: DMS-1108 explicitly owns strict relational-model compilation for stable-identity merge plans; plan compilation fails deterministically when a persisted multi-item collection scope lacks non-empty compiled semantic identity; and the failure happens because runtime chose the strict builder, not because DDL/default compilation was made globally strict.
steps-to-verify: Update DMS-1108 story text and tasks so strict relational-model compilation is an explicit prerequisite for emitting `CollectionMergePlan.SemanticIdentityBindings`.
When implemented, add runtime/plan tests that assert the strict builder path fails deterministically for missing semantic identity while the default builder remains permissive.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Update downstream stories that consume DMS-1108 so they rely on the explicit strict upstream contract instead of inheriting global validation through `CreateDefault()`. At minimum, revise `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03-persist-and-batch.md` to say runtime execution assumes DMS-1108 selected the strict relational-model pipeline and therefore received non-empty semantic identity for every persisted multi-item collection scope it merges. Acceptance: downstream write-path stories no longer rely on an implicit global validation side effect from DMS-1103; they point directly to DMS-1108 as the boundary that guarantees semantic-identity availability for executable collection merge behavior.
steps-to-verify: Inspect `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03-persist-and-batch.md` and confirm it names DMS-1108 or the strict builder contract as the upstream source of the semantic-identity guarantee.
Run: `rg -n "DMS-1108|strict|semantic identity" reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03-persist-and-batch.md`

description: Remove duplicated stable-identity primary-key construction for child collection tables. Consolidate the identical `BuildChildTableKey(DbTableName)` implementations currently in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/DeriveTableScopesAndKeysStep.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ExtensionTableDerivationPass.cs` into `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/RelationalModelStableIdentityHelper.cs` or another single shared helper. Acceptance: base and extension child-collection tables derive the same `CollectionItemId` PK shape from one implementation, no duplicate helper remains in the builder/pass codepaths, and existing manifests/fixtures remain unchanged except for harmless formatting.
steps-to-verify: Remove the local `BuildChildTableKey()` helpers from both `DeriveTableScopesAndKeysStep` and `ExtensionTableDerivationPass`, and update both callers to use the shared helper.

Run relational-model unit coverage that exercises both base and extension collection derivation, including `DeriveTableScopesAndKeysStepTests`, `ExtensionTableDerivationTests`, and `DbTableModelIdentityMetadataContractTests`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~DeriveTableScopesAndKeysStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionTableDerivationTests|FullyQualifiedName~DbTableModelIdentityMetadataContractTests"`

description: Reduce duplicated resource/extension traversal and mutation plumbing across semantic-identity compilation and array-uniqueness constraint application. Refactor `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/SemanticIdentityCompilationPass.cs` and `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ArrayUniquenessConstraintPass.cs` so the base-versus-extension resource walk, mutation lookup, and apply-back logic are shared rather than reimplemented in parallel flows. As part of the cleanup, remove dead parameters such as the unused `resourceModel` argument on `ApplyArrayUniquenessConstraint()`. Acceptance: pass ordering and behavior stay unchanged, semantic-identity precedence remains `arrayUniquenessConstraints` first then reference-derived fallback, no unused plumbing remains, and the refactored code makes the supported resource/extension mutation path explicit in one place.
steps-to-verify: Add or update regression coverage in `SemanticIdentityCompilationPassTests`, `SemanticIdentityPrecedenceTests`, and `ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests` to prove array-uniqueness-derived identity still wins over reference fallback and extension-contributed metadata still mutates the base resource.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~SemanticIdentityCompilationPassTests|FullyQualifiedName~SemanticIdentityPrecedenceTests|FullyQualifiedName~ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests"`

Run: `rg -n "ApplyArrayUniquenessConstraint\(|resourceModel," src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses` and confirm the unused `resourceModel` parameter plumbing has been removed.
